{{Quote|Our claim to these stars is natural and ancient. Our eyes have owned them since the beginning of our days. They are alive in our legends. They call to us in our dreams. We draw our strength from the All. The purity of the All inspires us to perfection. Our claim to these stars is not a shallow thing of greed, or politics, or ambition. It is not a claim we would ever surrender.|Nil Spaar|<ref name="Tyrant's Test">''[[Tyrant's Test]]''</ref>}}
The '''Duskhan League''' was a theocracy of the [[Yevetha]]n [[Species/Legends|species]] in the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]] that sat on the periphery of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The emblem of the government was a double circle of three-pointed stars on a scarlet background.<ref name="Tyrant's Test" />

==History==
The [[Yevetha]] Protectorate declared its independence as the Duskhan League during the [[Yevethan Uprising]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and was led for twelve years by [[Nil Spaar]] from the capital world of [[N'zoth]]. It consisted of a political theocracy of pure Yevethan colony worlds.<ref name="TEC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> By [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], the League included thirteen worlds in the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]], including [[N'zoth]], [[Wakiza]], [[Zhina]], [[Tizon]], [[Z'fell]], and [[Faz/Legends|Faz]].

In [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], the League forcefully colonized several worlds in the Koornacht Cluster after exterminating their non-Yevetha populations. A series of political and military engagements with the New Republic undertook a military intervention against the Duskhan League that became known as the [[Black Fleet Crisis]], culminating in the [[Battle of N'zoth|liberation]] of N'zoth by the New Republic in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]]. 

Under the New Republic, N'zoth was razed over a decade later by the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] during their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of the galaxy after the Yevetha began building up its [[Planetary Security Forces|planetary security forces]].

==Organization==
===Government===
{{Main|Darama}}
The Duskhan League was an [[theocracy]] that emerged in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] during the [[Yevethan Uprising]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and was led by the [[Darama]], a spiritual leader for the whole [[Yevetha]] that headed a strict hierarchical system and operated as the chief of state, and was treated with the greatest respect. Politically, Yevethan government was led by a Viceroy, who also operated as the prime minister for the Duskhan League. Departments of governance, commerce, science, and culture were led by [[Proctor (Yevethan)|Proctors]], and supported by various guilds. The Darama and Viceroy became over time linked positions, connecting Yevethan spirituality and politics.<ref name="Tyrant's Test" />

===Foreign policy===
{{Quote|I don't think there's any question but that Nil Spaar represents more worlds, a larger population, greater material riches, and a more advanced industrial-technological base than anyone who's come to Imperial City in the last twelve years. And he probably knows it, too.|Leia Organa Solo|<ref name="Before the Storm">''[[Before the Storm]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Black_Fleet_Crisis.jpg|thumb|250px|Nil Spaar meets [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]].]]
[[17 ABY/Legends|Thirteen years]] after the uprising, the isolationist regime opened talks with the New Republic by bringing a Yevethan delegation to Coruscant, but it was in truth a ruse to cause mistrust of [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] Leia Organa Solo in the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] and to gather intelligence on the New Republic. Nil Spaar rejected membership into the New Republic, instead proposing an alliance of sorts, despite all of Organa Solo's attempts to persuade him of the benefits of full membership. He used stories of Imperial brutality against the Yevetha to justify their wariness and desire for independence.<ref name="Before the Storm" />
